applied_optimizations.name: Triton kernel with reduced grid size
applied_optimizations.description: Reduced kernel launch overhead by using grid (batch_size * cdiv(spatial_size, BLOCK_SPATIAL),) instead of (batch_size, spatial_size). Each program now handles BLOCK_SPATIAL=256 spatial positions and loops over all 16 channels. This reduces programs from 1.6M to ~6400, achieving 1.095x speedup over PyTorch baseline.

attempted_but_failed.name: Full Triton implementation with custom InstanceNorm stats kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Variance computation in Triton kernel produced incorrect results (variance ~0.0172 vs PyTorch 0.0170). Two-pass mean/variance algorithm had precision issues.

attempted_but_failed.name: 2D block kernel with fewer launches
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton compilation error - unsupported tensor indexing with constexpr values

attempted_but_failed.name: Simple Triton kernel with (batch, spatial) grid
attempted_but_failed.reason: Correct but slower - 1.6M program launches had too much overhead

notes: Key insight: Reducing kernel launch count from 1.6M to 6400 programs was critical for performance
Each Triton program now handles 256 spatial positions and loops over 16 channels
Conv3d still dominates at 59.4% but cannot be optimized with Triton
Fused clamp+mult+max operations are now faster than PyTorch native ops when launch overhead is minimized

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trial 4 produced no output — agent has stalled 3 consecutive times trying complex InstanceNorm fusion. With 42 minutes left, give one final attempt with an extremely simplified approach: keep the work

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_5
attempted_but_failed.reason: Agent has produced no output for 4 consecutive trials attempting InstanceNorm fusion in Triton. With 42 minutes left on the last stage, giving one final attempt with an extremely constrained, copy-pas

global_notes: For elementwise fusion kernels, minimize launch overhead by having each program do more work
Grid size of (batch_size * cdiv(spatial_size, BLOCK_SPATIAL),) with BLOCK_SPATIAL=256 works well
Loop over channels inside kernel rather than having separate programs per channel
Conv3d dominates GPU time but is already well-optimized by MIOpen
